overview: The BMO Aggregate Bond Index ETF (ZAG.TO) seeks to replicate the performance of the FTSE Canada Universe Bond Index, which includes a diverse range of Canadian investment-grade bonds. The ETF holds government (federal and provincial), corporate, and municipal bonds, providing broad exposure to the Canadian fixed-income market. With an MER of just 0.09%, ZAG is one of the cheapest bond ETFs in Canada and is widely used as a core fixed-income holding in balanced portfolios. It employs a passive management strategy, aiming to track the index while minimizing tracking error.
bullCase: Ultra-low MER of 0.09% makes it one of the most cost-effective bond ETFs in Canada.
Provides broad exposure to the Canadian bond market across government and corporate issuers.
Offers regular income through interest payments, appealing to income-focused and conservative investors.
bearCase: Interest rate increases cause bond prices to fall, leading to potential capital losses.
May underperform in a rising interest rate environment compared to equities or short-duration bonds.
Limited growth potential compared to equity investments.
analystSummary: ZAG is the default Canadian aggregate bond ETF for most passive investors. Its 0.09% MER and broad FTSE Canada Universe Bond Index tracking make it a core fixed-income building block.
seoDescription: Explore whether ZAG is a good investment with our detailed analysis of the BMO Aggregate Bond Index ETF — 0.09% MER, broad Canadian bond exposure.
